items.title: Blackstone restricts flagship fund withdrawals as private asset fears reemerge
items.summary: Blackstone capped withdrawals from its $79 billiob BCRED fund after redemption requests rose from 7.9% to 10%, adding to private-market liquidity concerns.

items.title: Oil prices fall 3% on report Trump reluctant to restart Iran war
items.summary: President Donald Trump told aides that he won't resume the war unless U.S. troops are killed, according to The Wall Street Journal.

items.title: Not seeing any slowdown yet: Nykaa Fashion CEO
items.summary: Nykaa Fashion is now a significant driver for acquiring customers for Nykaa's beauty business. Both segments are experiencing robust growth. The company reported a substantial profit increase for the quarter ended FY26. Despite economic concerns, Nykaa sees no negative impact on consumer sentiment. The fashion business has expanded beyond women's wear into men's, kids, and home categories, showing accelerated growth.

items.title: Blackstone caps withdrawals from flagship private credit fund
items.summary: Redemption requests surged to $4.5bn in second quarter amid mounting fears in sector

items.title: Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles should aim for over 20% market share by 2030: Chairman
items.summary: With its sights firmly set on claiming more than 20 percent of the market by 2030, Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles is poised to invest Rs 35,000 crore in innovative product development. As a frontrunner in the electric vehicle arena, the company believes that proactive collaboration with dealer partners will be key to its upward trajectory.

items.title: Tax-cut hopes lift Indian bonds but RBI hike fears loom
items.summary: Reuters and ‌local media reported ⁠that ⁠the government is considering scrapping the 12.5% capital gains tax on overseas investors and the 20% withholding ​tax on interest earned by foreign investors in government securities.

items.title: Quotes of the day by Tom Russo: "I think investors should think more and trade less"
items.summary: Veteran investor Tom Russo advocates for thoughtful analysis and patience over frequent trading, highlighting that excessive activity incurs costs and emotional decisions. By focusing on high-quality businesses and allowing compounding to work, investors can achieve superior long-term wealth creation, especially during volatile market periods.

items.title: Amazon engineers in Seattle slam employer for building AI data centers while laying off 30,000 staffers
items.summary: Amazon engineers called out their employer for conducting mass layoffs while it commits to spending $200 billion this year on AI infrastructure.

items.title: Government rolling out 100 ethanol fuel stations in Delhi-NCR, Maharashtra: Hardeep Singh Puri
items.summary: India is expanding its network of ethanol fuel stations across Delhi-NCR, Pune, Mumbai, and Nagpur. This initiative aims to significantly reduce the country's reliance on imported fossil fuels. The government plans to increase the number of these stations to 500 by the end of 2026 and anticipates 5,000 by 2027. This move supports cleaner fuel alternatives and boosts farmer income.

items.title: Micron, Marvell, and Broadcom lead chipmakers' premarket losses
items.summary: U.S. chipmakers plunged in premarket trading after Broadcom earnings disappointed investors.

items.title: RBI clears Myntra's FEMA violations after penalty payment; ED closes investigation
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has closed an Enforcement Directorate investigation into Myntra Designs Private Limited. This action follows the RBI compounding certain Foreign Exchange Management Act violations. The company faced allegations of delays in submitting overseas investment reports and undertaking commitments before compliance. The RBI imposed a one-time monetary penalty, resolving the matter and concluding the probe.

items.title: Trump's 'big beautiful bill' has a 'double taxation' trap for top earners, tax lawyers say
items.summary: A new guide by Congress' tax policy staff indicates that trust income can be taxed twice.

items.title: US stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 futures drop up to 1% amid tech sell-off; Brent retreats
items.summary: US stock futures decline as technology rally halts on Broadcom's disappointing outlook, triggering broader sell-off. Ceasefire hopes boost Middle East negotiations, while SpaceX sets IPO at $135/share.

items.title: India remains among fastest-growing economies despite global uncertainty: Minister Prasada
items.summary: The minister highlighted India's push in trade, manufacturing, AI and semiconductors at IIFT's Global Business Research Conference.

items.title: Vodafone Idea among 4 stocks hit a 52-week high, rally up to 45% in a month
items.summary: The Great Wealth Shift: Where India’s Next Fortunes Will Be MadeVodafone Idea, Polycab India, CG Power and Federal Bank hit fresh 52-week highs, with Vodafone Idea leading the pack after a 45% rally in the past month.

items.title: Grest becomes first private firm to join govt IMEI verification platform
items.summary: Grest, a refurbished electronics platform, has partnered with C-DOT for the Sanchar Saathi initiative. This integration allows real-time verification of device authenticity against a government IMEI database. Grest is the first private company to go live on this system. This move aims to combat device theft and fraud in India.

items.title: Nifty holds ground ahead of RBI decision; Midcaps outshine
items.summary: The Sensex closed at 74,360.01, up 13.84 points or 0.02%, while the Nifty 50 settled at 23,416.55, gaining just 10.95 points or 0.05%

items.title: India's long-term story intact despite short-term headwinds: Citi CEO Jane Fraser
items.summary: Citi CEO Jane Fraser sees India's long-term growth story remaining strong. She highlighted India's reform momentum and digital transformation. Emerging technologies like AI will unlock significant opportunities. Fraser urged investors to focus on India's potential, not short-term challenges. Prime Minister Modi conveyed commitment to reforms and industry growth. India's digital progress is a striking change.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | US Stock Market Today | Live: Nasdaq futures lead declines after Broadcom results disappoint
Fire breaks out at Tata Steel's United Kingdom plant
items.summary: A fire erupted at Tata Steel's Port Talbot plant in the UK. Emergency services responded swiftly to the incident. All personnel were safely evacuated from the site. The fire occurred at a processing line. Tata Steel is transitioning to a low-carbon steel-making process. Though, iron making at the site ceased in October 2024.

items.title: All nine recent FTAs set to be operationalized within 10 months, says Goyal
items.summary: India expects the nine FTAs signed over the past three years to be operational within 9-10 months. Minister Goyal says these agreements complement India's growth strategy by expanding market access, facilitating trade an industry, enabling movement of talent and attracting investments.

items.title: Rajesh Exports chair asserts accuracy of financials after SEBI allegations
items.summary: The regulator’s observations stemmed from differences in revenue calculations ‌and did not consider ‌the ⁠company’s consolidated revenue, Chairman Rajesh Mehta ⁠said

items.title: Trent looks beyond apparel to focus on beauty, footwear
items.summary: The growing importance of these categories comes as some of Trent's international ventures face challenges.

items.title: Bluesky was launched as a Twitter rival — but it's far less popular. Now it's eyeing Reddit for inspiration
items.summary: "I think the public square is not the direction we want to go in...we're very inspired by companies like Reddit," Bluesky's Rose Wang told CNBC.

items.title: Broadcom headed for $300bn market value wipeout as revenue forecast disappoints
items.summary: Shares in chip company fall 15 per cent in pre-market trading on Thursday

items.title: Tire giant Pirelli vows legal action as short seller alleges ‘close relationship’ with Russia
items.summary: Pirelli has instructed lawyers to take legal action after short seller Grizzly Research claimed the firm has a "close relationship" with Russia.

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit: Nilesh Shah recommends 4 investment bets that should be part of your portfolio
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra AMC MD Nilesh Shah has identified four investment avenues for volatile markets: Special Investment Funds, performing credit AIFs, REITs and Gift City-based global products. He believes these options can help investors generate returns beyond traditional equity market gains while improving diversification.

items.title: India considers spending curbs to protect fiscal deficit target
items.summary: India's government is reviewing potential budget spending cuts. Higher oil prices are increasing subsidy costs and threatening fiscal goals. Officials are looking at trimming allocations in areas like water resources and loans to states. Capital expenditure and defense spending are unlikely to be reduced.

items.title: Lithium start-up EnergyX plans ‘Battery Mecca’ in Texas
items.summary: Project aims to disrupt China’s dominance of the supply chain

items.title: Withholding tax removal could unlock $25 billion in bond Inflows, says Citi's Aditya Bagree
items.summary: India anticipates substantial foreign investment. Two key policy shifts, removing withholding tax on government bonds and inclusion in the Bloomberg Global AGG Index, could attract billions. This influx would strengthen India's balance of payments and ease fiscal pressures. Citi believes these developments will boost the Indian economy, making it more attractive to global investors.

items.title: Voda Idea launches silent mobile verification for its subscribers using Meta platforms
items.summary: Vodafone Idea has introduced silent mobile verification for its users on WhatsApp, Facebook, and Instagram. This technology allows for password-less digital experiences. It verifies mobile numbers in the background without manual steps. Vi users will benefit from faster onboarding and enhanced security. This move aims to improve trust in digital services across India's growing digital ecosystem.

items.title: India-US trade deal could further boost already vibrant trade corridor: Citi India CEO
items.summary: A proposed India-US trade agreement is set to enhance trade and investment. Citi India CEO K Balasubramanian expressed optimism about this pact. He highlighted the strong India-US economic corridor, a key market for both nations. This agreement is anticipated to further invigorate existing trade. India's economic growth remains robust, outpacing many large economies globally.

items.title: India's ageing ammonia plants need upgradation, says industry body FAI
items.summary: India's fertiliser sector faces a challenge with its heavy reliance on imported natural gas. The Fertiliser Association of India is urging for improved efficiency and new technology in ammonia production. This is crucial for strengthening domestic fertiliser security. The industry is working towards better plant operations and maintenance.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex, Nifty end flat after weak open; Sensex up 14 pts, Nifty gains 11 pts
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Stock Price Highlights: After a negative opening, benchmarks ended flat. Sensex was up 13.84 pts or 0.02% to end at 74,360.01, Nifty 50 inched up 10.95 pts or 0.05% to 23,416.55.

items.title: RBI policy June 2026: How will the Indian stock market react to the MPC interest rate outcome?
items.summary: The six-member Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), chaired by RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra, began its three-day meeting on June 3 and will unveil its decision at 10 a.m. on June 5.

items.title: Rupee closes marginally weaker ahead of crucial central bank rate review
items.summary: The rupee closed at 95.7850 per US dollar, up from 95.7050 on Wednesday, fluctuating in a narrow range due to weaker Asian currencies and importer hedging following a central bank intervention

items.title: From inputs to retail, a monster rages through: As supply-side inflation pressures build, the implications for monetary policy
items.summary: India's inflation is shifting from producers to consumers, driven by cost-push factors like rising global commodity and energy prices. This is evident in the widening gap between WPI and CPI inflation. Subsequent retail fuel price hikes are expected to further increase transportation costs, impacting consumer prices across various sectors.

items.title: India-UK trade deal moving forward, though steel issue may delay launch
items.summary: Talks on a free trade deal between Britain and India are moving swiftly. Trade Minister Peter Kyle stated discussions are going well. While the deal itself will not be reopened, its implementation might occur later than initially planned. This is due to Indian concerns regarding upcoming British steel trade measures. The agreement was signed in July 2025.

items.title: The rise and rise of the IMF’s mysterious ‘other’ reserve currencies
items.summary: Sing me a song, Mr Reserve Manager

items.title: Go Zero ice cream registers 350% growth in May; founder Kiran Shah says, ‘Still not done with the season’
items.summary: Go Zero ice cream registered 350% growth in May, founder Kiran Shah revealed. This shows that health-conscious consumers are seeking alternatives to typical high-fat and high-sugar ice creams in this peak summer season.

items.title: Who is Elon Musk and what is his net worth?
items.summary: The boss of X, Tesla and SpaceX is the world's richest person and has used his platform to make his views known on a vast array of topics.

items.title: SoftBank shares plunge over 11% amid broader tech sell-off
items.summary: Shares of SoftBank fell 11% following an overnight sell-off in the U.S. market on the back of wider profit taking in the tech sector.

items.title: Central Bank of India targets over Rs 3,500 crore bad-loan recovery in FY27
items.summary: Central Bank of India plans significant recovery from bad loans exceeding Rs 3,500 crore in FY27. The bank will also introduce wealth management and credit card services in the latter half of the current fiscal year. Development of cash management services for corporate clients is also underway. These initiatives aim to boost the bank's financial performance and non-interest income.

items.title: Rupee closes at 95.78 vs USD ahead of crucial central bank rate review
items.summary: The Indian rupee declined for a second day ahead of the Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy review, with traders anticipating measures to support the currency. The rupee's recent recovery, aided by RBI intervention and expectations of boosted inflows, faces renewed pressure if Friday's policy fails to deliver.

items.title: Meesho’s AI discovery engine PRISM now drives over 75% of orders
items.summary: PRISM is designed around browsing and discovery behaviour

items.title: IEX shares in focus after May electricity trade volumes jump
items.summary: The exchange reported total electricity traded volume of 12,983 million units (MU) in May 2026 compared with 10,946 MU in the corresponding month last year

items.title: Market wrap: Sensex closes flat, Nifty holds 23,400; Titan, Eternal lead gains
items.summary: Indian stock markets finished the trading session with minor gains. The Sensex and Nifty saw fluctuations throughout the day before settling higher. India VIX, a measure of market volatility, experienced a notable decline. This indicates a period of relative stability after intraday movements. Investors observed the market's performance as it concluded the day's trading.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on June 4: Physicswallah, Anant Raj, Force Motors, Hitachi Energy, Voltas among top gainers
items.summary: The Nifty 50 and Sensex closed 0.14% and 0.03% higher at 23,396 and 74,368, respectively. The broader market, however, outperformed the benchmark indices, with both the Nifty Midcap 100 and Nifty Smallcap 100 indices rising 0.43% and 0.52%, respectively.

items.title: BRICS and the new geography of agri-trade
items.summary: India’s BRICS chairship in 2026 comes as agricultural trade faces new challenges beyond tariffs, including climate shocks, food-price volatility, fertiliser supply disruptions, sustainability requirements and digital traceability standards. With BRICS now accounting for nearly half the world’s population, around 40% of global GDP and $1.17 trillion in intra-group trade, agriculture has become a strategic area for cooperation.

items.title: Indri Whisky maker Piccadily launches travel retail-exclusive ‘Ilika’ at $80
items.summary: Ilika is matured in a combination of ex-Spanish Sherry and ex-American Bourbon oak casks at the company’s distillery near the Himalayan foothills

items.title: India set to implement 2-3 more FTAs in six months, says commerce minister Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India is set to implement at least two to three more free trade agreements within the next six months, with an additional three to four significant pacts expected by 2027. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal highlighted these developments, emphasizing the translation of negotiated agreements into commercial opportunities for businesses.

items.title: Physics Wallah looks to unwind FinZ lending bet days after ₹120 crore infusion
items.summary: The edtech company is exploring sale, loan-book transfer and licence surrender after abandoning plans to lend from its own balance sheet.

items.title: Sensex ends choppy trade flat ahead of RBI policy meet: 10 key highlights from Indian stock market today
items.summary: Indian stock market closes flat-to-positive amid cautious investor sentiment ahead of RBI meeting and global tensions.

items.title: Telcos urge TRAI to regulate OTT apps as spam shifts online
items.summary: Telecom firms report most spam now bypasses regulations via internet apps. They urge the regulator to bring these platforms under control. OTT providers counter that their oversight falls under a different ministry. Discussions are ongoing regarding new rules for spam calls and messages. Telecom operators are divided on proposed charges for robocalls.

items.title: SpaceX Set IPO Terms. How Orders for Its Massive Offering Will Work.
items.summary: This is tool banks such as Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley use to build an order book for the SpaceX IPO.

items.title: Trafigura warns oil at ‘inflection point’ as Iran war stokes bumper half-year profits
items.summary: Commodity trader’s net profit more than doubles to $4.1bn for October to March period

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit: Markets forget wars, don't stress too much on geopolitics, says Devina Mehra
items.summary: Devina Mehra advises investors to prioritize long-term portfolio construction over geopolitical events, citing over a century of market history where wars have had minimal lasting impact. She also stresses the importance of genuine global diversification, as market leadership frequently rotates between countries and sectors, cautioning against blindly investing in international products without due diligence.

items.title: India's viral 'Cockroach' party has millions of followers. What investors need to know
items.summary: A Gen Z-led, social media-first mock political party in India that has amassed millions of followers and is set to hold an in-person protest on Saturday.

items.title: Hitachi Energy, GVTD to CG Power: Citi is bullish on these electric equipment stocks. Should you buy?
items.summary: Citi estimates HVDC projects alone could represent an opportunity of around ₹1.6 trillion for equipment manufacturers. The brokerage also expects global transmission and distribution capital expenditure to exceed $15 trillion between CY25 and CY50.

items.title: Air India, Riyadh Air ink initial pact for codeshare partnership
items.summary: Air India and Saudi Arabia's Riyadh Air have signed an initial agreement. This partnership will offer seamless travel connections between India, Saudi Arabia, and beyond. Customers can expect a single booking and smoother onward journeys. The collaboration aims to support growing travel demand and explore joint initiatives. This move strengthens ties between the two nations.

items.title: Citi initiates coverage on 4 Indian power equipment stocks; sees up to 33% upside. Own any?
items.summary: Citi Research initiated coverage on four Indian electrical equipment makers, seeing strong domestic transmission buildout and accelerating HVDC adoption as key growth drivers. The firm issued 'Buy' ratings on Hitachi Energy India, GE Vernova T&D India, and CG Power and Industrial Solutions, with a 'Neutral' call on Siemens Energy, citing significant OEM opportunities and export potential.

items.title: Vedanta says no penalty, restrictions imposed after ED searches
items.summary: India's financial crime-fighting agency searched Vedanta group locations. The company stated no penalty or restriction has been imposed. Vedanta is cooperating with the foreign exchange law probe. Operations and financial activities remain unaffected. The searches were conducted in connection with a probe.

items.title: Rajesh Exports denies overstating revenues amid SEBI probe into ₹15.15 lakh crore gap, stocks hit lower circuit
items.summary: Slide follows SEBI’s interim findings that Rajesh Exports had prima facie misrepresented nearly ₹15.15 lakh crore in revenues linked to its subsidiaries between FY21 and FY25

items.title: Reliance traders said to game plan in case RBI raises rates
items.summary: Reliance Industries' treasury department is exploring strategies to manage its cash reserves amid potential interest rate hikes by the Reserve Bank of India. Traders are considering shifting funds to short-dated money market instruments, anticipating capital gains as yield spreads are expected to narrow. The company's discussions precede an upcoming RBI rate decision, with markets anticipating potential hawkish measures.

items.title: Partners Group warns it could cap more fund withdrawals after triggering private equity rout
items.summary: Partners Group said it could cap capital withdrawals from more of its funds after a European gating sparked a private equity stock sell-off on Wednesday.

items.title: Record pulses buffer to act as shield against El Nino, says govt official
items.summary: India holds a substantial 43 lakh tonnes pulses buffer that will protect against supply disruptions and price hikes due to a potential El Nino. The government assures this stock can be utilized if the weather phenomenon affects kharif sowing. This buffer is significantly larger than previous years, built through assured procurement policies.

items.title: IndiGo pips Air India again on overseas routes as West Asia crisis weighs
items.summary: IndiGo has reclaimed the top spot among Indian carriers on international routes in April, aided by a faster recovery from West Asia-related disruptions than the Air India group. Analysts expect the airline to retain its lead as it expands its overseas network.

items.title: ICICI Bank share price: Motilal Oswal sees 41% upside. Should you buy?
items.summary: Brokerage firm Motilal Oswal Services has projected a potential upside of 41% for ICICI Bank, has maintained a ‘buy’ rating on the stock and set a target price of ₹1,750 per share.

items.title: Dollar at two-month high as Gulf hostilities flare, yen near intervention zone
items.summary: Although Israel and ⁠Lebanon ⁠agreed to a ceasefire, a broader peace deal remained elusive, keeping oil prices elevated and supporting demand for the safe-haven ​dollar.

items.title: Meesho shares sink for eighth consecutive session in longest post-listing sell-off. What’s driving the decline?
items.summary: Meesho's shares continue to decline, dropping 5% to ₹166.60, wiping out 16.6% of its value. Lock-in expiry on June 9 may lead to significant selling pressure from early investors.

items.title: India to use artificial intelligence for machine-readable standards, says Consumer Affairs Secretary Khare
items.summary: India's Consumer Affairs Ministry is set to develop advanced, machine-readable standards using AI. This initiative aims to simplify compliance for industries. The Bureau of Indian Standards is transforming to support this modernization. The government is encouraging private sector testing infrastructure. Govt sets up 6 sector-specific groups to identify products to boost manufacturing, cut imports

items.title: India's data centre capacity could hit upper end of 5-8 GW target by 2030; Adani, Reliance best placed, says Bernstein
items.summary: India’s data centre capacity could expand to the upper end of the projected 5-8 GW range by 2030 from about 1.5 GW currently, according to Bernstein. The brokerage sees Adani Group and Reliance Industries as best positioned to benefit, citing their access to land, power infrastructure and transmission networks.

items.title: Delhi airport warns of weather disruptions; IndiGo, Air India advise passengers to check flight status
items.summary: Indira Gandhi International Airport in Delhi has alerted passengers about potential flight disruptions. Inclement weather has led major airlines like IndiGo, Air India, and SpiceJet to issue travel advisories. Flyers are urged to check their flight status before reaching the airport. The airport is working with stakeholders to minimize inconvenience.

items.title: El Niño needs monitoring, but India better prepared than earlier: Bayer’s Simon Wiebusch
items.summary: Farmers are increasingly recognising climate volatility as a long-term reality, driving greater adoption of climate-smart practices, says Simon Wiebusch.

items.title: Tata Communications stock rises 2%; new study exposes AI infrastructure gap in global enterprises
items.summary: The stock has gained over 25% in the past month and trades at a price-to-earnings ratio of 54.77, with a total market capitalisation of approximately ₹56,259 crore

items.title: Hindalco eyes Rs 1,000 crore revenue by FY29 with Eternia experience centre
items.summary: Hindalco Industries is targeting Rs 1,000 crore revenue by fiscal year 2029. This ambitious goal is driven by its Eternia brand, a fast-growing player in system aluminium windows. The company is expanding its partner network and manufacturing capabilities. A new experience centre in New Delhi marks a significant step.

items.title: SMR Jewels IPO allotment to be finalised today. Here's GMP and steps to check status
items.summary: According to platforms tracking the unofficial market, the grey market premium (GMP) for the SMR Jewels IPO is currently nil. Here's allotment status, latest GMP and IPO details.

items.title: Equity, Gold or Fixed Income: How should you invest amid stock market volatility? Here's a 50-40-10 strategy by MOSL
items.summary: On precious metals, the firm maintains a neutral allocation stance on both gold and silver, while assigning greater weight to gold. The preference for gold is supported by continued buying from central banks and easing speculative excesses in global gold markets.

items.title: Nifty faces key resistance near 23,450 as markets await policy outcome, says Ajit Nayak
items.summary: Indian stock markets experienced a mixed trading session as investors awaited the upcoming monetary policy. The Nifty is facing resistance near 23,450. Federal Bank shows promise above 301. The auto sector is gaining momentum with opportunities in Mahindra & Mahindra, Bajaj Auto, and Jamna Auto. Investors are watching macro events and sector-specific plays.

items.title: BIS studying implementation challenges for mandatory silver hallmarking, says DG
items.summary: Silver hallmarking has been voluntary since 2005 and from September 2025 onwards, hallmarked silver articles carry a Hallmark Unique Identification (HUID) number, enabling buyers to verify purity.

items.title: India should review FTAs, overhaul investment screening, report says
items.summary: India should reassess the effectiveness of its existing Free Trade Agreements (FTAs), modernise its investment screening framework and adopt a more coordinated trade and industrial policy approach as geopolitical tensions and economic fragmentation reshape global commerce, according to a report by Koan Advisory Group in association with Chintan Research Foundation, Information Technology Industry Council and Institute of Chinese Studies.

items.title: Silver drops in futures trade on weak global cues, inflation worries
items.summary: On the MCX, the white metal for July delivery depreciated by ₹621, or 0.24%, to ₹2,62,337 per kg in a business turnover of 11,830 lots.

items.title: PE wars to billion-dollar IPO dreams, hospital sector is fighting fit
items.summary: Major private equity firms are vying for a stake in Cloudnine, a leading maternity and paediatric care chain. This intense competition highlights strong investor confidence in India's healthcare sector. The industry is entering a new growth phase driven by increasing demand, a shortage of hospital beds, and a rise in specialized medical services.

items.title: Govt sets up 6 sector-specific groups to identify products to boost manufacturing, cut imports
items.summary: India is boosting domestic manufacturing. Six expert groups will identify 100 products to reduce import dependence. This initiative aims to promote local production for both Indian and global markets. The move also seeks to curb foreign exchange outflow. These groups will submit their final list within three weeks. This will strengthen India's economy.

items.title: BIS plans phased move towards mandatory silver hallmarking
items.summary: The Bureau of Indian Standards is studying mandatory hallmarking for silver. This move aims to ensure purity for buyers. Silver hallmarking is more complex than gold due to its diverse market. BIS is proceeding cautiously to set systems right before implementation. Mandatory hallmarking will be introduced gradually. The number of hallmarked silver articles is increasing.

items.title: India a strong long-term bet despite everything, says BofA India chief
items.summary: Global investors see India as a strong long-term investment choice; despite market ups and downs, confidence in India's economy remains high, says BofA India CEO Sahu. According to him, manufacturing expansion offers significant investment potential.

items.title: US economy remains strong, India must accelerate reforms and AI adoption: Ajay Srivastava
items.summary: Market veteran Ajay Srivastava highlights the U.S. economy's robust performance, contrasting it with Indian investors' perceptions. He emphasizes the global economy's resilience despite geopolitical tensions and urges India to focus on its own economic development and embrace global diversification, particularly in AI adoption.

items.title: Maruti Suzuki share price gains after it unveils Wagon R as India’s first flex-fuel vehicle
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki share price has fallen over 3% in one month and has declined 7% in three months. The auto stock has dropped 21% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis, while it has risen 8% in one year. Maruti Suzuki stock price has gained 38% in three years.

items.title: Vahh Chemicals IPO: Issue subscribed 77% so far on first bidding day. Check GMP, issue details
items.summary: The Vahh Chemicals IPO opened for subscription on Thursday, 4 June and will close on Monday, 8 June. The SME issue is priced at ₹60 per equity share, with a face value of ₹10 per share. Vahh Chemicals IPO GMP is ₹11.

items.title: Tamil Nadu signs Rs 18,600 crore MoU with Larsen & Toubro for data centre, shipbuilding projects
items.summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister V Joseph Vijay witnessed a significant MoU signing. Larsen & Toubro Limited will invest Rs 18,600 crore in three major projects. These projects will boost data centres, electronics manufacturing, and shipbuilding. Around 8,200 jobs will be created. This marks a key step towards Tamil Nadu's economic growth and industrial development.

items.title: U.S. proposes fresh tariffs on 60 economies over forced labor trade practices
items.summary: USTR has proposed a 10% duty rate for economies that have adopted a full or partial prohibition on forced labor trade, and 12.5% for all other economies.

items.title: Who is Rajesh Mehta, man behind Rajesh Exports, facing SEBI ban over alleged Rs 15.15 lakh crore 'fraud' impacting LIC, others?
items.summary: Market regulator SEBI has taken action against Rajesh Exports and its Chairman Rajesh Mehta. The company faces allegations of significant financial misstatements and fund diversion. SEBI's order points to a pattern of non-genuine transactions and questionable accounting. This action raises serious concerns about the company's financial reporting and corporate governance practices.

items.title: Top 10 small-cap mutual funds that gave up to 14% returns in 2026 so far
items.summary: For the year, the Nifty Smallcap 100 index is up 2.46% and remains in the green for the third straight month, showing signs of resilience. April was the best month for the index with an 18% rise. The index added 0.73% in May and is flat with a positive bias in June so far.

items.title: SAS Mumbai comeback hits turbulence as inaugural flight turns back to Copenhagen
items.summary: SAS's return flight to India faced a setback. The inaugural Copenhagen to Mumbai flight turned back mid-air. This happened because final regulatory approval was not received as expected. SAS is working to resolve the issue. The airline expects to start services soon. This route is important for connecting Scandinavia and India.

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit: A 12% return is a really good job in markets, says HSBC MF CEO Kailash Kulkarni
items.summary: HSBC Mutual Fund CEO Kailash Kulkarni urged investors to keep return expectations realistic, saying a 12% annual return is a strong outcome in equity markets. Speaking at the ET Alpha Wealth Summit, he highlighted export-led manufacturing as a key long-term opportunity and said AI could empower retail investors through better access to information.

items.title: Pahlaj Nihalani, producer and former CBFC chief, dies at 76
items.summary: Veteran producer and former Central Board of Film Certification chairperson Pahlaj Nihalani has died. Nihalani began his career in 1982 and is credited with launching the careers of actors Govinda and Chunky Panday. His tenure as CBFC chief from 2015 to 2017 was marked by controversy. He is survived by his wife and three sons.

items.title: RBI MPC: A problem Shaktikanta Das had contained may be returning to haunt Sanjay Malhotra & Co
items.summary: India's central bank, the RBI, confronts a new inflation challenge. Rising oil prices and supply disruptions threaten to undo recent progress. Policymakers are now considering a potential rate hike. This comes as the monsoon forecast also raises concerns about food prices. The RBI's focus shifts to managing inflation expectations amidst global uncertainties.

items.title: Gold rises in futures trade as investors seek safety amid global uncertainty
items.summary: Analysts said renewed investor interest in precious metals was driven by lingering geopolitical concerns and softer crude oil prices, which improved sentiment towards gold prices

items.title: Vibhor Steel Tubes approves wholly-owned subsidiary for infrastructure steel products
items.summary: Viyom Steel Infra will focus on steel products for infrastructure use, including transmission towers, monopoles, crash barriers and octagonal poles

items.title: Mint Explainer | Why has the US proposed fresh tariffs on India?
items.summary: Experts have argued that India should not fall for the pressure tactics. It should keep the latest Section 301 tariffs and bilateral trade agreements separate.

items.title: 13 midcap stocks rally up to 55% in just 3 months. Do you own any?
items.summary: Despite a nearly 6% decline in the Nifty 50 over the past three months, the Nifty Midcap 150 saw a 3.5% gain. Thirteen midcap stocks stood out, delivering impressive returns between 30% and 55%, demonstrating significant opportunities within this segment even as broader indices faced pressure.

items.title: Asia-Pacific markets fall on renewed Middle East tensions
items.summary: Asia-Pacific markets open lower amid renewed tensions between Iran and the U.S. and worries that the conflict may keep inflation elevated.

items.title: Bond yields soften ahead of RBI policy; how should debt market investors position amid repo rate hike fears?
items.summary: Indian rupee has depreciated by more than 5%, while the the benchmark 10-year bond yield has risen 34 bps since the US-Israeli war on Iran began on February 28.

items.title: Avoid chasing IT rally, focus on power and steel plays: Neeraj Dewan
items.summary: Market expert Neeraj Dewan advises selective investing amid volatility, favoring power, energy infrastructure, steel, aviation, commercial vehicles, FMCG, and select auto ancillaries. He remains cautious on IT due to weak guidance. Dewan sees opportunities in renewable energy and battery storage, and believes oil price stabilization will aid aviation stocks.

items.title: RBI MPC meeting: Here's the date, time, and where to watch Governor Sanjay Malhotra's address live on June 5
items.summary: The six-member Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), led by RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra, commenced its three-day deliberations on June 3 and is scheduled to reveal its policy outcome at 10 a.m. on June 5.

items.title: PhysicsWallah scraps in-house lending plan, stock surges 15%
items.summary: The strategic shift, aimed at reducing risk and improving scalability, lifted the stock over 15% in intraday trade.

items.title: Rupee dips; RBI-led relief may fade without inflow measures, bankers say
items.summary: The Indian Rupee declined on Thursday, continuing a two-day fall. Weaker Asian currencies and importer hedging impacted the Rupee. The Reserve Bank of India's intervention had previously supported the currency. Future RBI policy actions will be crucial. Asian currencies also weakened amid global tensions.

items.title: Merritronix IPO share allotment likely today: Latest GMP, steps to check allotment status online for SME IPO
items.summary: Investors can check the Merritronix IPO allotment status online either through the BSE website or via Bigshare Services, the registrar to the issue.

items.title: Global Markets: Japan's Nikkei pulls back from record high on AI selloff
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei index experienced its biggest drop in three weeks on Thursday. This pullback followed a record high. Investors sold AI-related stocks after Broadcom's disappointing revenue. Renewed Middle East conflict also impacted market sentiment. SoftBank Group saw a significant decline. Despite the broader market fall, Tokyo Electron and Disco shares rose, providing support.

items.title: Amit Khurana backs LTIMindtree, OFSS and eClerx as tactical bets
items.summary: Amit Khurana of Dolat Capital has upgraded India's IT sector, citing depressed valuations and rupee depreciation as key drivers. He believes the market is underpricing these tailwinds, projecting double-digit net earnings growth for IT exporters by FY27. Khurana also highlighted four specific IT stocks he is backing, including LTIMindtree, OFSS, eClerx Services, and Intellect Design Arena.

items.title: Partners Group prepares to cap withdrawals at US fund for wealthy individuals
items.summary: Swiss group faces increasing strains as redemption requests from investors rise

items.title: Why this Maldives hotel chain is chasing India’s leisure destinations
items.summary: Maldives-based resort operator Atmosphere Core is expanding aggressively in India with nearly 30 signed hotel projects, focusing on leisure destinations such as the Northeast, Odisha and Goa

items.title: Markets claw back losses by midday; IT drags, energy and pharma gain
items.summary: The recovery marks the third consecutive session in which the market has bounced from the 23,250–23,150 support zone, a pattern analysts at SBI Securities flagged as significant

items.title: Inox Wind's stumble may be setting the stage for a strategic transition
items.summary: Weak earnings, muted order inflows and missed guidance hurt sentiment, but a shift toward equipment supply contracts and captive demand could support Inox Wind's turnaround.

items.title: India fights a familiar fear even as it keenly watches new US tariff move
items.summary: India 12.5% new tariff: Indian exporters are watching US tariff reviews closely, hoping that trade talks will protect their market share. A uniform tariff could hurt India's advantage as buyers shift from China. India's strong labour laws are expected to help in negotiations.

items.title: West Bengal to sign MoU with Adani Ports, eyes fresh investment push
items.summary: West Bengal government has signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Adani Ports. This comes after a meeting between Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari and Adani Ports & SEZ MD Karan Adani. The Chief Minister assured support for Adani's investments in the state. Adani Group had previously pledged Rs 10,000 crore investment in West Bengal.

items.title: Hero MotoCorp shares rise 3% as firm unveils India's first 100cc flex-fuel motorcycles. Check details
items.summary: Hero MotoCorp shares gained 3%, after launching India's first flex-fuel motorcycles, the Splendor+ and HF Deluxe, supporting sustainable mobility and India's transition to cleaner fuels. These 100cc bikes are compatible with E20 to E85 ethanol blends, aiming to reduce carbon footprint and crude oil imports. The company plans a phased nationwide rollout starting July 2026.

items.title: 'India to pilot hydrogen buses, trucks on 10 roads; Reliance, Tata, NTPC among partners,' says Nitin Gadkari
items.summary: India is launching a pilot project for hydrogen-powered buses and trucks on ten roads. Major companies like Reliance and Tata are partnering in this initiative. The country aims to become an energy exporter, moving beyond imports. This marks a significant step in India's energy transition, with flex-fuel vehicles also set for expansion.

items.title: Toyota Innova Crysta 2026 launched with new upgrades: Check variant-wise prices, features and other details
items.summary: Toyota Kirloskar Motor has launched the updated 2026 Innova Crysta in India, featuring cosmetic and feature enhancements. This likely marks the final major refresh for the model before its discontinuation around 2027. The updated MPV starts at Rs 19.72 lakh, ex-showroom, and retains its 2.4-litre diesel engine.

items.title: Today’s Gold Rate in India June 4: Gold prices down in Ahmedabad, Mumbai, Kolkata, Bengaluru, Delhi
items.summary: 22, 24 carat Gold prices down across Indian cities on June 4

items.title: Indian corporations poised to become large brand owners in US: Oyo founder Ritesh Agarwal
items.summary: Indian companies are poised to become significant brand owners in the United States. This shift will enhance India's global influence beyond Bollywood and IT. Technology is a key driver for this expansion. Ritesh Agarwal, founder of Oyo-parent firm PRISM, believes India's moment has arrived. Indian brands will soon be used and appreciated by American consumers.

items.title: SpaceX says it's worth $1.75tn as it targets largest stock market debut
items.summary: Elon Musk’s space exploration company set a target share price for buyers earlier than expected.

items.title: Nayara Energy completes scheduled Vadinar refinery turnaround
items.summary: Nayara Energy has finished its scheduled maintenance at the Vadinar refinery. This critical work involved thousands of personnel and heavy equipment. The refinery, India's second-largest, is now back online. Nayara ensured fuel supplies remained uninterrupted across its network during the shutdown. The company focused on safety, efficiency, and reliability. This project strengthens India's energy security.

items.title: Titan, Kalyan, Vedanta among stocks in focus after silver import restrictions
items.summary: Jewellery stocks saw mixed movement on Thursday after most of them dipped in the previous trading session

items.title: Sensex recovers 700 points from day’s low, Nifty rises above 23,450. 5 key factors behind market rebound
items.summary: Indian stock markets staged a strong comeback, with Sensex and Nifty recovering significantly from morning lows. Falling oil prices, a strengthening rupee, and potential scrapping of capital gains tax on FPI investments in government securities boosted investor confidence. Broader markets also saw a sharp rebound, indicating positive sentiment despite recent foreign investor outflows.

items.title: Physicswallah shares rally 18%, snap 5-day losing streak. What's behind the surge?
items.summary: PhysicsWallah shares rose sharply on Thursday, adding nearly Rs 5,000 crore in market value after the company reversed its lending strategy and partnered with regulated NBFCs for student loans. The move, aimed at reducing balance sheet and credit risks, helped the stock snap a five-session losing streak.

items.title: Maruti unveils India's first flex-fuel car: What is a flex fuel vehicle? How does it work? Will it cut your fuel bill? Here's all
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki has introduced India's first flex-fuel passenger car. This technology allows vehicles to run on petrol or petrol-ethanol blends. It is seen as a significant step towards reducing crude oil imports. The move also aims to lower carbon emissions and enhance India's energy security. This innovation could unlock substantial benefits for the country.

items.title: Petrol, diesel price hikes in India remain 'very minor' compared with global peers: Hardeep Singh Puri
items.summary: India's fuel price hikes are minor globally, with the nation ranking second lowest. This stability stems from a focused energy strategy. The government balances energy security with affordability. India is promoting ethanol blending and flex-fuel vehicles. This transition is structural, accelerating cleaner energy adoption across millions of vehicles. Such moves align with India's long-term energy roadmap.

items.title: Hexagon Nutrition IPO opens June 5: 10 key things to know before subscribing; check GMP today
items.summary: The company, which aims to raise ₹139 crore from the issue, has fixed a price band of ₹42-45 per share. The IPO will be open from 5 June to 9 June.

items.title: SoftBank’s AI-Fueled Rise: Opportunity, euphoria and emerging risks
ICICI Bank shares fall 10% in 6 months. Here’s why Motilal Oswal sees 41% upside potential
items.summary: Motilal Oswal Financial Services maintains a 'Buy' rating on ICICI Bank, projecting a 16% loan CAGR and sustained profitability despite recent stock underperformance. The brokerage highlights the bank's robust asset quality, strong liability franchise, and cost leadership, anticipating a significant upside potential of 41% with a target price of Rs 1,750.

items.title: Will India’s economy be in trouble if IMD’s monsoon forecast proves accurate?
items.summary: With the IMD predicting the weakest monsoon in over a decade, economists warn that the threat to growth hinges on specific regional risks, not just the headline numbers.

items.title: Europe unveils tech sovereignty package amid growing concerns over reliance on U.S. tech: 'We want to be sure nobody has a kill switch'
items.summary: The proposals include new acts to bolster advanced chip manufacturing and homegrown cloud computing.

items.title: Texmaco, Touax and TrinityRail form railcar leasing venture in India
items.summary: A new partnership is set to boost railcar leasing in India. Touax Group and TrinityRail Global Inc are joining forces with Texmaco Rail & Engineering Ltd. This collaboration aims to offer advanced railcar designs and leasing solutions. It will combine manufacturing, leasing, and financing capabilities. The venture will support India's growing freight rail market.

items.title: Physicswallah share price jumps 18% after partnership with NBFCs for student financing
items.summary: PhysicsWallah share price surged 18% as it shifts to partnering with NBFCs for student lending, aiming to reduce balance sheet exposure and credit risks. The company will connect students with compliant lending partners to enhance affordability and scalability.

items.title: Jefferies initiates coverage on Poonawalla Fincorp with Buy rating. Why are analysts bullish?
items.summary: Jefferies has initiated coverage on Poonawalla Fincorp with a Buy rating and a target price of Rs 490. The brokerage sees strong growth potential driven by a new leadership team and an expanded product range. Poonawalla Fincorp is expected to achieve the fastest AUM growth among major NBFCs. Profitability is also projected to improve significantly, supporting premium valuations.

items.title: Govt moves to standardize edible oil pack sizes, industry sees boost for consumer transparency
items.summary: The Indian government is set to introduce new rules for edible oil packaging. This move aims to standardize pack sizes, making it easier for consumers to compare prices. Manufacturers believe this will lead to fairer competition and better transparency. The initiative also focuses on sustainable packaging practices. Consumers will benefit from clearer choices and increased confidence in packaged edible oils.

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit: India outperformed emerging markets over a 10-15 year period; market lag is recency bias: Vikas Khemani
items.summary: Veteran investor Vikas Khemani said concerns over Indian equities stem largely from recency bias, not weakening fundamentals. Speaking at the ET Alpha Wealth Summit, he highlighted India’s strong long-term outperformance versus emerging markets and urged investors to focus on structural growth drivers rather than short-term market volatility.

items.title: NSE SME stock Simca Advertising jumps despite muted trends on Dalal Street
items.summary: Simca Advertising share price opened at ₹134.50 apiece today, as compared to previous close of ₹136.15 on Wednesday.

items.title: Maruti Suzuki launches India's first flex-fuel car, bets on biofuels to boost energy security
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki has launched India's first flex-fuel passenger car, the Wagon R. This move aims to significantly cut crude oil imports and lower carbon emissions. The technology supports farmers and rural communities, linking agriculture with clean mobility. This initiative is a key step towards India's energy security and self-reliance. The company is committed to cleaner mobility solutions.

items.summary: Global stocks are under pressure despite oil easing after a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, while investors anticipate SpaceX's IPO.

items.title: Earnings slowdown in FY27? JM Financial lists 5 sectors which must do the heavy lifting
items.summary: Nifty 50 earnings growth fell short of expectations in FY25 and FY26. While Q4 FY26 results led to an upward revision for FY27E Nifty 50 EPS growth, past downgrades suggest caution. Automobiles, metals, NBFC, telecom, and infrastructure are poised for strong growth. Private banks form the backbone of these expectations.

items.title: UBS downgrades BHEL shares to Neutral from Buy. Check target, key reasons
items.summary: UBS has downgraded BHEL to "Neutral" from "Buy", citing a more balanced risk-reward outlook after the stock's strong run, though it raised the target price to Rs 460 from Rs 375, implying about 14% upside. The brokerage believes much of BHEL's order book growth is already reflected in the stock price.

items.title: More oil escapes Hormuz, keeping traders guessing
items.summary: Iran war: Tankers are using stealth to exit the Strait of Hormuz, freeing oil trapped in the Gulf. The practice of ships going dark is widespread. This makes it harder to gauge market flows. Oil on water in the Gulf has dropped. This shows outflows are inching higher. The market may not return to normal.

items.title: Reliance Industries dividend record date tomorrow: Last chance to buy Mukesh Ambani-led stock
items.summary: Reliance Industries has set June 5 as the record date for its final dividend of ₹6 per share for FY26, making June 4 the last day for investors to buy the stock and qualify for the payout.

items.title: Indian consumers turn defensive as job fears rise, economic confidence falls
items.summary: Indian consumers are increasingly concerned about jobs and the economy. This is leading many to reduce spending on non-essentials like dining and shopping. People are prioritizing savings and financial security. Despite these worries, travel plans remain strong, with many intending to take domestic and international holidays. Consumers are also planning for once-in-a-lifetime experiences.

items.summary: Law firms are ramping up their advisory practices as litigation work dries up. Sebi probes are now more rigorous and clients prefer settlements over tedious litigation. Demand is rising for preventive compliance checks and governance reviews, prompting firms to deploy more lawyers for advisory.

items.summary: On Tuesday, Microsoft announced a host of developer features for its Windows OS, alongside seven new artificial intelligence (AI) models, a new security initiative, in-house chips and an agentic operating system (OS) designed for future AI-first devices.

items.title: Norms issued to estimate District Domestic Product
items.summary: New uniform guidelines for estimating district domestic product have been released by the statistics ministry, aiming for standardized indicators and bottom-up data collection. This move, aligning with the revised 2022-23 national GDP base year, will enhance decentralized planning and evidence-based policy formulation across all key economic sectors at the district level.

items.title: We expect interest rates to be left the way they are: Ficci chief Anant Goenka
items.summary: Ficci president Anant Goenka advocates for steady interest rates, citing supply-side price pressures from the West Asia crisis. He suggests restricting rupee intervention to volatility management and believes foreign capital will return after market corrections, while emphasizing proactive engagement with FTAs and focus on sunrise sectors for manufacturing.

items.title: OECD sees India growth slowing to 6.3% from 7.6% in FY27
items.summary: India's economy faces a slowdown to 6.3% in fiscal 2027. Higher energy costs from the Middle East conflict will impact investment and exports. Despite this, India remains a top global growth engine. Private consumption and investment are expected to moderate. Inflation will rise before easing. Global growth also slows.

items.title: Thali costs climb in May: Crisil report
items.summary: Home-cooked vegetarian and non-vegetarian thalis saw a 5% and 7% price increase in May, respectively, due to rising costs of tomatoes, vegetable oils, and LPG. Tomato prices surged 57% year-on-year, with further increases expected due to production declines and heat-related concerns. Potato and onion prices are also anticipated to rise, while pulses are expected to remain stable.

items.title: FirstClub raises $55 million to deepen quality-first premium quick commerce play

items.title: India-Oman trade pact creates strong export push for India's textile MSMEs: Govt
items.summary: India's textile and apparel sector is poised for significant growth in Oman following the India-Oman Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA). The pact grants immediate duty-free access on 945 textile and apparel lines, eliminating a 5% MFN duty. This move is expected to boost India's exports, valued at $95.

items.title: Gulf states in talks for oil pipelines to bypass Hormuz
items.summary: Oil exporters seek alternative ways to export, as crucial waterway has been disrupted for over three months

items.title: Dulux owner Akzo Nobel closes 17% lower after takeover talks collapse; European stocks fall
items.summary: Investors are also monitoring developments in the U.S.-Iran war after tensions escalated overnight.

items.title: Bata India bets on improving inventory reduction through efficiency steps, omni-channel strategy
items.summary: Bata India emphasizes inventory reduction through efficiency, with a 13% decrease in gross inventory in the latest quarter. The company's omni-channel strategy and product expansion plans signal future growth despite challenges.

items.title: Fuel price shield helping India and consumers to tackle West Asia crisis: Gita Gopinath
items.summary: India is already cushioning the economy from the West Asia crisis. Gita Gopinath, former IMF Deputy Managing Director, states the government is limiting fuel price hikes. This acts as an implicit subsidy. She advises more targeted support for vulnerable households and businesses instead of broad fuel subsidies. This approach helps where needed most and avoids economic distortions.

items.title: Former UBS private credit fund says law firm helped defraud it of $145mn
items.summary: Fund alleges Pillsbury Winthrop lawyer sent it ‘fake financials’ to secure loans linked to Aspiration Partners

items.title: Guwahati Tea Auction Centre sets new benchmark for quality tea prices
items.summary: Guwahati Tea Auction Centre achieved record prices for quality tea. Deckiajuli Tea Estate sold premium CTC leaf at Rs 1,103 per kg. Sotai Tea Estate tea also reached a new high of Rs 889 per kg. Over 65,000 kg of tea sold above Rs 400 per kg. The Assam Government offers subsidies for orthodox tea.

items.title: Partners Group limits withdrawals at private equity fund for wealthy individuals
items.summary: Swiss private capital firm caps redemptions from its $8.6bn flagship vehicle

items.title: Why FirstCry excelled at selling diapers but hasn’t quite cracked the pre-school code
items.summary: Parents buy diapers online, but choose preschools offline. FirstCry thought its vast baby product experience and ecosystem would help it crack the adjacency of preschools. Six years on, Intellitots is profitable but not even halfway near its 1,000-school ambition.

items.title: Israeli startup Coralogix raises $200 mn, to allocate 20% to India biz
items.summary: Coralogix, which had entered India in 2020, had announced that it would invest $30 million over three years into the country.

items.title: Tweaks to the BoJ’s balance sheet plans in June could follow bond volatility
items.summary: Bank of Japan has been slowly reducing its bond purchases to speed up quantitative tightening efforts

items.title: Elevated oil prices could drag India's growth to 6%, below IMF forecast of 6.5%: Gita Gopinath
items.summary: Elevated oil prices could slow India's economic growth to around 6%, according to former IMF official Gita Gopinath. She warned that prolonged West Asian tensions could further impact global growth and India's prospects. Gopinath emphasized supply-side reforms, renewable energy, and improving ease of doing business to boost investment and strengthen India's global supply chain role.

items.title: Sebi flags ₹15 trillion misstatement in Rajesh Exports accounts
items.summary: Sebi has alleged that ₹15.15 trillion in revenue was misrepresented, and flagged gaps in disclosures and inconsistencies in filings. It has also barred promoter Rajesh Mehta from trading in the shares of Rajesh Exports until further orders.

items.title: US announces new tariffs over forced labour concerns
items.summary: It comes after the US Supreme Court struck down many of US President Donald Trump's previous duties in February.

items.title: MoSPI releases uniform norms for DDP estimates with 2022-23 base year​
items.summary: The Statistics Ministry has released uniform guidelines for compiling District Domestic Product (DDP) estimates with a 2022-23 base year. These guidelines, finalized after stakeholder consultation, aim to ensure consistency and comparability in district-level economic data. The framework elaborates on concepts, data sources, and estimation procedures, emphasizing a bottom-up approach where possible.

items.title: ADB, StanChart ink partnership to support Indian firms across supply chains
items.summary: The Asian Development Bank and Standard Chartered Bank have partnered to boost supply chain finance in India. Agreements include risk-sharing for USD transactions via GIFT City and a guarantee facility for rupee transactions, aiming to expand access for businesses and enhance trade flows.

items.title: Trump says Iran agreed to not have nuclear weapons, but 'they can change their mind'
items.summary: The president was speaking to the New York Post's "Pod Force One" podcast in a wide-ranging interview.

items.title: Lloyds, Halifax and Bank of Scotland back after online banking outage
items.summary: "We're aware some customers are having issues with our app and online banking. We're really sorry about this," Lloyds Bank posted on X.

items.title: Bitcoin hits lowest since February as crypto competes for liquidity with blockbuster IPOs
items.summary: Bitcoin continues to decline as stocks hit record highs, with investors also eyeing big IPOs.

items.title: RBI to hold rate on Friday, future hike will depend on inflation data: Gita Gopinath
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is likely to keep interest rates unchanged in the near term and adopt a data-dependent approach before taking any future policy action, according to Gita Gopinath, former First Deputy Managing Director of the International Monetary Fund (IMF).

items.title: Universal park officially named as government pledges £1.3bn
items.summary: The government's contribution is going towards upgrading local infrastructure and transport links.

items.title: Alphabet's $80 billion stock sale leaves Wall Street in 'unprecedented territory,' says Goldman's Gutman
items.summary: Goldman Sachs is acting as a joint book-running manager for Alphabet's underwritten offerings.

items.title: Cabinet clears ₹20,000 cr schemes for jet fuel support, cleaner Delhi vehicles
items.summary: One-time budgetary support of up to ₹10,000 crore for OMCs to provide ATF price stabilization amid high crude prices. The budgetary support will be in the form of interest-free advances to OMCs.

items.title: Kotak Alts raises ADIA-anchored $1 bn real estate fund
items.summary: Kotak Alts has closed its 14th real estate fund at $1 billion with major investments from Abu Dhabi's ADIA and NPS Korea. This marks the latter's first commitment in Indian alternative assets.

items.title: How have crude oil prices behaved during the West Asia war?
items.summary: The West Asia war pushed Brent crude from about $70 to a peak of $126 per barrel, raising fuel costs and inflation in India, which imports 90% of its oil. Prices have eased amid Iran-US peace talks but remain elevated, keeping pressure on growth, consumers and businesses.

items.title: Apple agrees to provide financial details of India operations to CCI in antitrust case: What it means for iPhone maker
items.summary: The matter represents one of Apple's most prominent regulatory challenges in India, a strategically important market where the company has significantly expanded iPhone manufacturing as part of its efforts to reduce dependence on China.

items.title: France's Macron invites Sam Altman to attend G7, OpenAI tells CNBC
items.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has looked to win over tech bosses amid hopes to boost AI capability.

items.title: OECD warns of global slowdown as U.S.-Iran war stymies economic growth prospects
items.summary: The OECD has cut its global growth outlook, warning of lasting damage caused by the Middle East conflict.

items.title: Life after IPL: how JioHotstar plans to keep viewers and advertisers hooked without cricket
items.summary: The streaming platform is rolling out AI tech, niche content, and ad features to keep the traffic it gained during the tournament from disappearing.

items.title: India-US trade deal weeks away as negotiators resolve 99% of issues, says US Ambassador Sergio Gor
items.summary: US Ambassador Sergio Gor said negotiators have resolved 99% of outstanding issues and expect an interim trade agreement within weeks, with only technical matters left to be finalized.

items.title: Nestlé India flags shaky demand from urban middleclass as incomes lag
items.summary: India’s consumption recovery could face a fresh test as the urban middleclass has entered a ‘wait and watch’ mode on discretionary spending as its income growth lags inflation, says Nestlé India CMD Tiwary. The demand from the premium and rural sectors has, however, shown resilience, he says.

items.title: Perplexity CEO tells CNBC one metric will determine who wins the AI race
items.summary: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas said that whichever company can provide the "most taken value per watt per user" will be the AI winners in the future.

items.title: Suzlon’s next growth bet: solar, batteries and a tight grip on capital
items.summary: The company’s ‘Suzlon 2.0’ strategy aims to turn it into a one-stop renewable energy solutions provider spanning wind, solar and battery storage, while staying capital-light and reviving exports.

items.title: Larry Ellison beats Google's Sergey Brin, Amazon's Jeff Bezos to 3rd spot on billionaire rich list as Oracle stock soars
items.summary: Tech mogul Larry Ellison has jumped to the third spot on Bloomberg's billionaire rich list, overtaking Google's Sergey Brin and Amazon's Jeff Bezos for the position, as his company Oracle's stock soars.

items.title: True North seeks exit from Cloudnine Hospitals amid $300 million fundraise
items.summary: The fundraising exercise comes weeks after Kids Clinic India, which operates Cloudnine Hospitals, agreed to acquire the maternity, childcare and fertility businesses of Apollo Health

items.title: India’s services sector maintains growth momentum in May as output expands: HSBC India Services PMI
items.summary: The HSBC India Services Purchasing Managers’ Index, compiled by S&P Global, rose to 59.8 in May from 58.8 in April. It has continued to grow since April, after registering its weakest growth in 14 months in March.

items.title: Publishers in UK can opt out of Google AI search results
items.summary: The Competition and Markets Authority says it would put publishers "in a stronger position to negotiate content deals with Google".

items.title: Big Tech's AI ambitions pose a major power test for Europe
items.summary: Japan's SoftBank on Saturday announced plans to build 3.1 GW of AI data centers in the northern Hauts-de-France region by 2031.

items.title: Indian IT eyes return to on-site model, but with a talent and cost twist
items.summary: From the world’s largest banks to retail majors, customers now increasingly prefer engineers who combine AI expertise with sales and consulting skills to drive end-to-end deployment of the new technology.

items.title: Microsoft doubles down on Windows, developers as US tightens AI oversight
items.summary: Microsoft put up a bullish stance despite the US government’s decision, which will require all tech companies to give the US government access to frontier AI models 30 days before releasing them commercially.

items.title: China is making it harder for mom and pop to access U.S. stocks. Here's who will benefit
items.summary: Chinese regulators recently tightened scrutiny on offshore brokerages including Futu and Tiger Brokers.

items.title: Large brands gain as West Asia war, GST cut hit auto aftermarket’s small players
items.summary: Last year’s GST cuts have narrowed the price gap between formal automotive spare parts and cheaper alternatives sold by unorganized players, accelerating the shift towards formal brands.

items.title: Three-quarters of workers not on track for 'moderate' pension income, report suggests
items.summary: A newly-published report suggests a moderate lifestyle in retirement costs £32,700 for one person and £45,400 for two.

items.title: The 18 creatures in the running to be on the new banknotes
items.summary: The Bank of England is asking the public which animals should appear on future banknotes.

items.title: Microsoft testing wearable AI gadget aimed at office workers
items.summary: The company said its own workers are testing a "wearable access badge" and a desktop device.

items.title: Microsoft says new quantum chip 1,000 times more reliable than predecessor
items.summary: The tech giant predicts it will have a quantum computer that can solve commercially useful problems by the end of the decade.

items.title: Water firm fined £1.8m over parasite outbreak
items.summary: Four people were hospitalised and there were more than 140 cases of sickness and diarrhoea in Devon.

items.title: King told me Post Office scandal was 'dreadful', says oldest victim
items.summary: Betty Brown says she is accepting the honour on behalf of all the victims of the scandal.
